steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three verbs describing the temperature across three months: "is 62.6 degrees in June," "[___] to 66.6 degrees in July," and "dips slightly to 62.6 degrees in August." The blank must complete a parallel series, so the verb must match the tense and form of "is" and "dips."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has risen': present perfect — different tense from "is" and "dips," breaks the parallel. ✗
  • Choice B — 'rises': simple present, same tense and form as "is" and "dips" — parallel series is complete. ✓
  • Choice C — 'rising': present participle, not a finite verb — cannot serve as the main verb here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rose': simple past — shifts tense, breaks the parallel. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
